mnemonics: Java is Platform Independent: Think of Java as a universal traveler – it packs its suitcase (bytecode) once and can unpack and run anywhere with a JVM (Java Virtual Machine) hotel!
OOP Principles: Remember the four pillars with APIE – Abstraction, Polymorphism, Inheritance, Encapsulation.
keyPoints: Java is both compiled (to bytecode) and interpreted (by JVM).
Java applications are platform-independent but JVM is platform-dependent.
The main() method is the entry point of any Java application.
Java supports automatic garbage collection for memory management.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to declare the main method as public static void main(String[] args).
Using == for string comparison instead of the .equals() method.
